title: Sandbox Code Execution (E2B)
description: Agent executes code and shell commands inside an isolated E2B sandbox environment

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Configures SandboxAgent, E2BSandboxClientOptions, and triggers Runner.run()

actors.name: SandboxAgent
actors.description: AI agent with sandbox capabilities; drives tool calls inside the sandbox

actors.name: Runner
actors.description: Orchestrates run loop; creates SandboxRuntimeSessionManager per run

actors.name: E2B Platform
actors.description: Remote cloud sandbox provider; exposes Sandbox, CodeInterpreter APIs via e2b / e2b-code-interpreter SDKs

use_cases.name: Configure Sandbox Options
use_cases.description: Instantiate E2BSandboxClientOptions with sandbox_type (e2b | e2b_code_interpreter), template, timeout, exposed_ports, workspace_persistence (tar | snapshot), on_timeout (kill | pause), mcp, pause_on_exit

use_cases.name: Create Sandbox Session
use_cases.description: SandboxRuntimeSessionManager calls E2BSandboxClient.create(snapshot, manifest, options) → _sandbox_create(SandboxClass, template, ...) → E2B Platform creates sandbox → wraps in E2BSandboxSession → SandboxSession
use_cases.method: async create(snapshot, manifest, options: E2BSandboxClientOptions) -> SandboxSession

use_cases.name: Resume Sandbox Session
use_cases.description: Deserialize E2BSandboxSessionState from run state; call E2BSandboxClient.resume(state) → _sandbox_connect(SandboxClass, sandbox_id) to reattach or recreate sandbox

use_cases.name: Start Sandbox
use_cases.description: session.start() → _prepare_backend_workspace() creates workspace root via mkdir, materializes Manifest (files/dirs), installs runtime helper scripts

use_cases.name: Execute Shell Command
use_cases.description: Agent invokes exec_command tool → ExecCommandTool.run(ExecCommandArgs) → session.exec(cmd, shell, user) → E2BSandboxSession._exec_internal() → _sandbox_run_command() → E2B commands.run() → returns ExecResult(stdout, stderr, exit_code)
use_cases.method: async run(args: ExecCommandArgs) -> str  [args: cmd, workdir, shell, login, tty, yield_time_ms, max_output_tokens]

use_cases.name: Execute Command with PTY (Interactive)
use_cases.description: When tty=true or session.supports_pty(): ExecCommandTool → session.pty_exec_start(*command, shell, tty, user, yield_time_s) → _sandbox_pty_create() + poll PTY output → returns PtyExecUpdate(output, exit_code, process_id)
use_cases.method: async pty_exec_start(*command, tty, yield_time_s, max_output_tokens) -> PtyExecUpdate

use_cases.name: Write to Process Stdin
use_cases.description: Agent invokes write_stdin tool → WriteStdinTool.run(WriteStdinArgs) → session.pty_write_stdin(session_id, chars, yield_time_s) → _sandbox_pty_send_stdin() → returns updated PTY output
use_cases.method: async run(args: WriteStdinArgs) -> str  [args: session_id, chars, yield_time_ms, max_output_tokens]

use_cases.name: Read File from Workspace
use_cases.description: session.read(path, user?) → _sandbox_read_bytes via E2B files.read(path, format='bytes') → returns io.IOBase stream

use_cases.name: Write File to Workspace
use_cases.description: session.write(path, data) → _sandbox_write_bytes via E2B files.write(path, bytes) → WorkspaceArchiveWriteError on failure

use_cases.name: Resolve Exposed Port Endpoint
use_cases.description: session._resolve_exposed_port(port) → _sandbox_get_host(sandbox, port) → _e2b_endpoint_from_host(host) → ExposedPortEndpoint(host, port, tls). Port 443 uses TLS, others do not. Raises ExposedPortUnavailableError if host is invalid

use_cases.name: Stop / Pause Sandbox
use_cases.description: session.stop() → if pause_on_exit: _sandbox_pause(sandbox) else _sandbox_kill(sandbox). Used during SandboxRuntimeSessionManager.cleanup() after run completes

use_cases.name: Create Workspace Snapshot
use_cases.description: During session stop with workspace_persistence='snapshot': sandbox.create_snapshot() → encodes snapshot_id with magic prefix _E2B_SANDBOX_SNAPSHOT_V1. On 'tar' mode: workspace is tar-archived

use_cases.name: Manage Session Lifecycle (Acquire / Ensure / Cleanup)
use_cases.description: SandboxRuntimeSessionManager: acquire_agent(agent) prevents concurrent reuse; ensure_session() creates or resumes session; cleanup() calls stop/shutdown/delete and serializes resume state

use_cases.name: Serialize / Deserialize Session State
use_cases.description: serialize_resume_state() builds payload {backend_id, current_agent_key, session_state, sessions_by_agent}. E2BSandboxSessionState holds sandbox_id, sandbox_type, manifest, snapshot, exposed_ports, base_envs, timeouts, on_timeout, auto_resume

use_cases.name: Delete Sandbox Session
use_cases.description: E2BSandboxClient.delete(session) called during _SandboxSessionResources.cleanup() after stop/shutdown to release remote E2B resources

key_relationships: Developer configures E2BSandboxClientOptions and SandboxRunConfig, then calls Runner.run(SandboxAgent, input, run_config)
Runner creates SandboxRuntimeSessionManager; calls acquire_agent → ensure_session → returns BaseSandboxSession
SandboxRuntimeSessionManager uses E2BSandboxClient (BaseSandboxClient) to UC02 Create or UC03 Resume session
E2BSandboxClient wraps E2BSandboxSession in SandboxSession via _wrap_session()
E2BSandboxSession extends BaseSandboxSession; calls E2B Platform APIs (_E2BSandboxAPI: commands, files, pty)
Shell Capability provides ExecCommandTool + WriteStdinTool bound to BaseSandboxSession
ExecCommandTool uses session.supports_pty() to choose between pty_exec_start (PTY) and exec (one-shot)
SandboxAgent.run_as → User is injected into Manifest.users and passed as user= to exec calls
Two E2B sandbox types: E2BSandboxType.E2B (bash) and E2BSandboxType.CODE_INTERPRETER (Jupyter/IPython)

key_types.E2BSandboxClientOptions: BaseSandboxClientOptions: type='e2b', sandbox_type, template, timeout, exposed_ports: tuple[int,...], workspace_persistence: 'tar'|'snapshot', on_timeout: 'kill'|'pause', pause_on_exit, base_envs, mcp, auto_resume, timeouts: E2BSandboxTimeouts
key_types.E2BSandboxSessionState: SandboxSessionState: sandbox_id: str, sandbox_type: E2BSandboxType, manifest: Manifest, snapshot, exposed_ports, base_envs, timeouts, on_timeout, auto_resume
key_types.ExecResult: dataclass: stdout: bytes, stderr: bytes, exit_code: int, ok() -> bool
key_types.ExposedPortEndpoint: frozen dataclass: host: str, port: int, tls: bool=False, query: str='', url_for(scheme) -> str
key_types.ExecCommandArgs: BaseModel: cmd, workdir, shell, login, tty: bool, yield_time_ms: int=10000, max_output_tokens
key_types.WriteStdinArgs: BaseModel: session_id: int, chars: str, yield_time_ms: int=250, max_output_tokens

error_types: ExecNonZeroError - command returned non-zero exit code
ExposedPortUnavailableError - port not reachable or host invalid
WorkspaceStartError - failed to create workspace root
WorkspaceArchiveReadError - file read from sandbox failed
WorkspaceArchiveWriteError - file write to sandbox failed
WorkspaceReadNotFoundError - file not found in sandbox
WorkspaceWriteTypeError - incorrect type for write operation
important_notes: E2B_API_KEY env var required for all E2B sandbox operations
e2b and e2b-code-interpreter Python packages are optional extras; imports are lazy
Two sandbox types: 'e2b' (bash/shell interface) vs 'e2b_code_interpreter' (Jupyter/IPython REPL)
Workspace persistence: 'tar' serializes workspace to tar archive; 'snapshot' uses native E2B snapshot API with magic prefix _E2B_SANDBOX_SNAPSHOT_V1
SandboxAgent cannot be reused concurrently across runs (_SandboxConcurrencyGuard enforces this)
PTY support: pty_exec_start creates interactive TTY sessions; write_stdin interacts with running PTY process by session_id
ExposedPortEndpoint: port 443 → TLS=True; others → TLS=False; url_for(scheme) generates http/https/ws/wss URLs
Manifest root defines the workspace root directory; all paths resolved relative to it
run_as user is injected into Manifest.users and passed to exec calls as sudo -u <user> -- ...
Session resume: backend_id must match; sessions tracked per-agent by stable resume key (agent.name)
